creator: ruben@verweg.com
text:
Hi Philip,

On 26 Jan 2009, at 19:39, Philip M. Gollucci wrote:

> Ruben van Staveren wrote:
>> I see that in the meantime a new port has appeared, too bad they  
>> didn't contact the maintainer of sysutils/linux-megacli
>> I think it is confusing for end users of the original port to see  
>> it disappear and then reappear by another maintainer without a  
>> clear reason. There might be some fixes in this port sysutils/linux- 
>> megacli2 does not have. I leave it to portsmgr@ on which port  
>> continues to exist.
> I was under the impression from the previous PR (thats my bad for  
> not double checking before committing it) that 1.x and 2.x were for  
> different cards.

No it is the same binary, port was marked broken for a while and I  
went through some length to see whether the new version was supported  
by all cards as I had some reports from users with slightly different  
cards. Well, my testers didn't respond or didn't had the time and  
testing on my perc 5i (inside a 2950 1st gen) didn't show regression  
so I went ahead with the update.

> I'm a big fan of the Dell PowerEdges and perc 5i/6i/6e

LSI/Dell should support a FreeBSD version of that binary. I thought bz@freebsd.org 
  tried to convince them about that.

>
>
> They are also seeing use at the Apache Software Foundation so I'm  
> very interested in sorting this out.

It is quite interesting you know ? poking around too much with megacli  
can lock up the controller under 6.x. (seen it with 6.2 atleast, not  
seen under recent 6.4) and yet people have no other way of managing  
these systems.

Other big change in this version is that after reading LSI's EULA we  
can't really auto fetch the distfile too...

Since LSI pulled the 1.x version there is no need to have distinct  
linux-megacli/linux-megacli2 ports. I don't believe controllers have  
been dropped from being supported. Yet the warning using a non-native  
binary using all kinds of emulation layers to manage mission critical  
systems stands :)

Cheers,
	Ruben
